  • [St. Louis] 2 dead, 9 still missing after Washington paper mill tank implosion and officials say there's no hope of survivors · 2026-05-27
    Officials say the tank that imploded at a paper mill in Washington state is at risk of collapsing further and leaking more of the caustic liquid.
  • [St. Louis] New U.S. quarter honoring America’s 250th birthday begins shipping next month · 2026-05-27
    The 2026 semiquincentennial quarter features Thomas Jefferson and the Liberty Bell as part of the nation’s upcoming 250th anniversary celebration.
  • [St. Louis] How much does the winner of the Scripps National Spelling Bee get? The answer may surprise you · 2026-05-27
    In addition to a cash prize, the winner will also receive a trophy, a medal and several other prizes.
  • [St. Louis] Longtime St. Charles County elected official dies after cancer battle · 2026-05-27
    Scott Shipman's family said no service is planned, but a celebration of life will be announced at a later date.
  • [St. Louis] Man accused of sexually assaulting 13-year-old girl at Kirkwood Walmart · 2026-05-27
    Police said the man took the 13-year-old girl to an isolated corner of the store and assaulted her.
  • [St. Louis] Colorectal cancer screening guidelines updated to include blood test for the first time · 2026-05-27
    According to the American Cancer Society, about one in three adults who are eligible for screenings are not tested.
  • [St. Louis] What is Eid al-Adha and why do Muslims say 'Eid Mubarak'? · 2026-05-27
    Known as the Festival of Sacrifice, Eid al-Adha is the second of the two major Eid holidays celebrated by Muslims each year.
  • [St. Louis] US weapons stockpile being drained by Iran war will take years to replenish, analysts warn · 2026-05-27
    The depleted weapons systems are Tomahawk cruise missiles, along with Patriot and THAAD interceptors that defend against incoming missiles and drones.
  • [St. Louis] Multiple homes engulfed by Idaho brush fire; 6 officers injured while evacuating residents · 2026-05-27
    Idaho authorities said six law enforcement personnel were injured while assisting with evacuation efforts. The fire engulfed multiple homes in Mountain Home, Idaho.
  • [St. Louis] 'Emily in Paris' actor Pierre Deny dies after 'sudden' case of ALS · 2026-05-27
    Deny appeared in seasons three and four of the hit Netflix streaming show as Louis de Leon, a French luxury goods executive.
  • [St. Louis] Tesla in autopilot mode crashes into pond, killing 87-year-old driver: Florida Highway Patrol · 2026-05-27
    The Florida Highway Patrol said the crash happened when a 87-year-old was driving a Tesla Model Y SUV while it was in autopilot.
  • [St. Louis] YouTube to start automatically labeling some AI-generated videos · 2026-05-27
    New labels for AI-generated content will appear directly on videos and Shorts as the platform expands efforts to flag realistic AI-created media.
  • [St. Louis] St. Louis police looking for missing 28-year-old woman with autism · 2026-05-27
    Anyone with information about Tafari's whereabouts is asked to call St. Louis police at 314-444-1212 or dial 911.
  • [St. Louis] What is a free kick? Understanding soccer's most common action · 2026-05-27
    They are among the most common actions in soccer. Here's what they are:
  • [St. Louis] Missouri birth control access bill clears legislature after years of obstacles · 2026-05-27
    Right now, women on Medicaid can access an annual supply of birth control. This law would expand the same parameters to include private insurance.
  • [St. Louis] Man shot, killed north of downtown St. Louis · 2026-05-27
    The shooting happened at about 10:50 a.m. Tuesday on North 13th Street.
  • [St. Louis] Former US Attorney General Pam Bondi is undergoing treatment for thyroid cancer · 2026-05-27

    This market will resolve to "Yes" if a US-initiated drone, missile, or air strike on the soil of Colombia is announced or credibly reported to have occurred by the listed date ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No". For the purposes of this market, a qualifying "strike"
  • [Polymarket] Will Anže Logar be the next Prime Minister of Slovenia? · 2026-05-28
    Parliamentary elections are scheduled to be held in Slovenia on March 22, 2026. This market will resolve to the next individual who is officially elected by the National Assembly (Državni zbor) and sworn in as Prime Minister of Slovenia following the next parliamentary election.
  • [Polymarket] Will Microsoft be the second-largest company in the world by market cap on May 31? · 2026-05-28
    This market will resolve to the second-largest company in the world by market cap on May 31, 2026, as of market close. The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible reporting.

  • [Polymarket] Israeli parliament dissolved by May 31? · 2026-05-28

  • [Polymarket] Will Jared Hudson be the Republican nominee for Senate in Alabama? · 2026-05-28

  • [Polymarket] Xi Jinping divorce before 2027? · 2026-05-28

  • [Marginal Revolution] Wednesday assorted links · 2026-05-27

  • [Marginal Revolution] Seven ways to avoid losing your job to AI · 2026-05-27

  • [Conversable Economist] Lessons for Central Banks: Interview with Kristin Forbes · 2026-05-27

  • [Conversable Economist] How Medicare and Medicaid Rely on Private Health Insurance · 2026-05-26
    For a majority of Medicare and Medicaid recipients, the government program is paying a private health insurance company to provide the service from a private firm. In the Spring 2026 issue of the Journal of Economic Perspectives, Tim Layton, Luca Maini, and J. Michael McWilliams
  • [Conversable Economist] Agricultural Production Functions Without Prices · 2026-05-22
